summary: The Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture remains open in its full generality, including the well-known case $r = n/3$ (existence of a directed triangle). Post-2013 work has produced only partial results: improvements on the digraph triangle bound, proofs for restricted classes (e.g., small independence number, forbidden subgraphs), counterexamples to a stronger conjecture of Thomassé, and progress on Aharoni's rainbow generalization (now resolved up to a constant factor and up to an additive constant).

since_posted.title: On the length of directed paths in digraphs
since_posted.claim: Disproves Thomassé's strengthening of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture for all girth $g \geq 4$ and proves new lower bounds on directed path lengths in digraphs of given minimum out-degree and girth.

since_posted.title: Improved bounds for the triangle case of Aharoni's rainbow generalization of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture
since_posted.authors: Patrick Hompe, Zishen Qu, Sophie Spirkl
since_posted.claim: Establishes improved triangle-existence bounds in Aharoni's rainbow generalization of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture, e.g. that $(1.1077, 1/3)$ is triangular and $(1, 0.3988)$ is triangular.

since_posted.title: Further approximations for Aharoni's rainbow generalization of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture
since_posted.claim: Proves Aharoni's rainbow version of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture holds when each color class has size $\Omega(k)$, improving the previous $\Omega(k \log k)$ requirement and bringing the result to within a constant factor of the conjecture.

since_posted.title: Aharoni's rainbow cycle conjecture holds up to an additive constant
since_posted.claim: Shows that Aharoni's rainbow generalization of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture holds up to an additive constant: edge-colored graphs with $n$ vertices and $n$ color classes of size at least $r$ contain a rainbow cycle of length at most $n/r + \alpha_r$.

since_posted.title: Short rainbow cycles in edge-colored graphs
since_posted.authors: Xiaozheng Chen, Shanshan Guo, Fei Huang
since_posted.claim: Proves that for edge-colored graphs on $n$ vertices with $n$ color classes (each a matching of size 2 or a triangle) of which at least $\alpha n$ have a given form for $\alpha > 1/2$, the graph contains a rainbow cycle of length $O(\log n)$, contributing to Aharoni's rainbow version of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture.

search_queries: Caccetta Haggkvist conjecture minimum outdegree girth digraph arxiv
Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture n/3 triangle digraph recent results
notes: The original Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture is unresolved, including the central r = n/3 (triangle) case. Most recent activity post-2013 concerns either Aharoni's rainbow generalization or strengthenings/restrictions (small independence number, forbidden subgraphs). The 2024 Cheng-Keevash paper disproves a strictly stronger conjecture of Thomassé but does not affect the original Caccetta-Häggkvist statement. The SIAM page for Devlin/Aharoni-style 'Nonuniform Degrees' rainbow paper returned 403 and was not directly verified, so it is not cited.
